text:
Once upon a time in the small, snowy corner of Scotland...

There lived a curious fox, busy exploring every inch of his forest home. His favorite spot? Under the ancient oak tree where he'd stash his stash of honey cakes.

Every day, he'd drift off dreaming about adventure. His escapism was easy—he'd just imagine himself flying. He'd playfully toss a pawful of honey into the air, imagining how sweet the world would look from winged heights.

One particularly bright winter morning, fluffy clouds shaped like squirrels giggled as they danced. The youngest squirrel, Olive Olive, fluttered past the sleeping fox.

'Ooooh,' she gigged, 'I wouldn't want to fly like that. I could never climb that high...' She paused dramatically.

'I know just the thing! You want a grand adventure?' the fox said, eager.

'I love mysteries! I could solve the oldest mystery book in town… Why the fox likes dancing."

The fox buzzed excited
